Sil..personality: A yielding, mild, and gentle temperament that lacks stamina and firmness, much like a stalk of grain without its silica coating. The patient is timid, retiring, and wants to shirk responsibilities, yet when forced into action can perform with surprising competence and accuracy.
Sil..mental_state: Marked by a profound dread of failure and fear of undertaking any task, despite possessing the actual ability to succeed. The mind is weakened from prolonged mental effort, producing a brain-fag characteristic of professional and studious persons — clergymen, lawyers, students — who have exhausted themselves through sustained intellectual work and sleepless nights.
Sil..emotional_pattern: Deeply anxious and lacking self-confidence, with an underlying irritability and irascibility that emerges when provoked, though the default state is one of tearful mildness and gentle withdrawal. Religious melancholy, sadness, despondency, and a pervasive sense of embarrassment and self-consciousness dominate, with the patient feeling their own selfhood so acutely that they cannot lose themselves in their work or public duties.
